item.createcybernetics.data_shard_infolo:
Welcome to Cybernetics.

Cyberware is installed through a Surgery Table (by a Ripperdoc) or a Robosurgeon. Most implants require a compatible body part, an available installation slot, and sufficient Humanity or Data Integrity.

§bENERGY§r
Energy powers active cyberware. It can be generated or stored by compatible implants, such as generators, batteries, and other energy-producing systems.

If your available energy supply is too low, powered implants may shut down and stop functioning until enough energy is restored. Maintain a reliable energy source before relying on energy-intensive cyberware.

§6HUMANITY§r
Humanity represents your remaining psychological tolerance for cybernetic augmentation. Installing cyberware reduces Humanity, and excessive augmentation can push your mind beyond safe limits.

Once Humanity falls below 25%, you begin experiencing Cyberpsychosis. The further Humanity falls, the more severe the condition becomes.

Humanity can be restored by either removing implants, or using Neuropozyne, a psychoactive immunosuppressant that targets rejection. It is brewed with Datura Flowers, an ancient plant obtainable from archaeological digs and Sniffers. To increase the duration of the effect, use an Autoinjector.

§dDATA INTEGRITY§r
Data Integrity replaces Humanity after replacing your brain with a Cerebral Processing Unit.

Unlike Humanity, Data Integrity is not reduced by installing implants. Instead, it gradually declines while you remain awake. Sleeping allows your Cerebral Processing Unit to perform a system-wide reset and regenerate your mind files.

Failing to sleep for too long will lower Data Integrity. Like Humanity, dropping below 25% begins the onset of Cyberpsychosis.

§4CYBERPSYCHOSIS§r
Cyberpsychosis has three escalating levels.

§eLEVEL 1 — Below 25%§r
Mild visual and auditory disturbances begin to affect the subject.

§6LEVEL 2 — Below 15%§r
Visual and auditory disturbances intensify. The subject may also experience sluggishness, nausea, and occasional physical damage.

§cLEVEL 3 — Below 0%§r
The subject experiences intense visual and auditory disturbances, sluggishness, nausea, and occasional damage.

At this level, the subject may enter Cyberpsychotic Fugue States: periods of lost self-control marked by violent and unstable behavior. These episodes become more severe as Humanity or Data Integrity falls further into negative values.

Maintain your energy supply. Monitor your Humanity or Data Integrity. Sleep when required. A neglected cybernetic system will eventually become a threat to its owner.

The §3Robosurgeon§r automates the surgery process with precision, speed, and consistency.

By opening the interface, the user is able to move between implantation slots to mark installs, removals, and replacements to be completed by the §3Robosurgeon§r.

To mark an item for §3install§r, load the implant into the desired and relevant slot. If the slot turns blue, the item has been successfully marked for installation. To unmark the item for installation, simply click on it.

To mark an item for §4removal§r, click on a currently installed item. If the slot turns red, the item has been successfully marked for removal. To unmark the item for removal, simply click on it again.

To §5replace§r a current install with a new item, place the new item directly into the currently occupied slot. If the slot turns red and the new item takes the place of the old one, the item has successfully been marked for replacement.

Be cautious not to remove §cvital organs§r. Removal of default organs WILL have §ldisastrous§r effects.

Some implants require others to be installed, or are incompatible with others. If this occurs, the item will be ejected from the Robosurgeon.

To set up the Surgery Chamber, place the Robosurgeon directly above the Surgery Chamber, then supply power to it.

   §c[SUPPLY POWER]§r
         §3,__,__,
         §3|__,__|
         §3|__,__|§r   <----  §3ROBOSURGEON
         §3|__,__|
         §a|   |   |
         §a|   |   |§r   <----  §aSURGERY CHAMBER
         §a|   |   |
         §a|   |   |
         §a|   |   |
         §a|__|__|§r

To begin the surgery, walk into the §aSurgery Chamber§r and close the doors behind you.

When the surgery is complete, the §3Robosurgeon§r will play a §o'ding'§r sound. Opening the doors before the surgery is concluded will cancel it.

You are now ready to begin implanting cyberware by yourself!

§1FBCs§r (A.K.A.: §1Full Body Conversions§r) are sets of implants that fully convert the user's physical body to be better suited for specific functions, like combat, mining, stealth, etc. To apply an §1FBC§r, install the required implant set. These sets can be modified to fit your needs better by adding more implants, but uninstalling items from the required set will remove the §1FBC§r.
This is different than a §dTBC§r (A.K.A.: §dTotal Body Conversion§r), which is instead a total replacement of all biological parts.

This document catalogs known cybernetic organisms encountered across the overworld, the Nether, and hostile settlements. Entries are based on field observation, recovered implant data, and post-encounter anatomical analysis.

The listed subjects should be considered unstable unless otherwise noted. Cybernetic augmentation does not inherently produce aggression, but trauma, incompatible hardware, failed maintenance, neurological degradation, and hostile programming frequently result in dangerous behavior.

____________________________________

§lCYBERZOMBIE
§oClassification: Undead Cybernetic §oHuman
§oThreat Level: §6Moderate
§oDisposition: §4Hostile

Cyberzombies are deceased or reanimated human subjects whose bodies retain enough functional cybernetic hardware to remain mobile after biological death. Their implants continue to supply mechanical assistance to damaged musculature, maintain limited coordination, and compensate for organ failure that would otherwise render the subject immobile.

Unlike conventional zombies, Cyberzombies exhibit abnormal durability and more consistent locomotion. This is likely caused by reinforced skeletal components, artificial muscle bundles, and residual power stored within implanted systems. Their behavior remains largely instinctive: they pursue living targets, respond to nearby movement, and attack through blunt force rather than deliberate tactical behavior.

Field observations suggest that many Cyberzombies were not originally intended for combat. Their augmentation appears to be civilian, industrial, medical, or survival-oriented hardware repurposed by post-mortem reanimation. Despite this, their implants make them significantly more dangerous than typical undead.

____________________________________

§lCYBERSKELETON
§oClassification: Undead Cybernetic §oHuman, Advanced Decomposition State
§oThreat Level: §4High
§oDisposition: §4Hostile

Cyberskeletons are heavily decomposed Cyberzombies whose external tissue has deteriorated to the point that only skeletal structure, mechanical implants, and surviving connective hardware remain. Their lack of skin and muscle tissue exposes much of their cybernetic framework.

Despite appearing more fragile than Cyberzombies, Cyberskeletons are often more dangerous. Their remaining body mass is lower, allowing implanted mechanical systems to move the subject with greater speed and efficiency. Many specimens also retain ranged-combat capability, suggesting that their neural remnants or embedded targeting systems preserve more complex combat behaviors than those found in ordinary undead.

____________________________________

§lSMASHER
§oClassification: Heavily Augmented §oIllager
§oThreat Level: §5Extreme
§oDisposition: §4Hostile

Smashers are Illager combat subjects transformed through extreme cybernetic augmentation. Their bodies have been rebuilt around heavy industrial and military-grade implants, producing immense physical strength, impact resistance, and close-range destructive capability.

A Smasher’s skeletal system is reinforced beyond normal biological limits, while artificial musculature and hydraulic assist systems allow it to strike with crushing force. Most observed specimens possess heavily modified arms, reinforced torsos, and extensive neurological hardware intended to suppress pain response and increase aggression.

The psychological condition of Smashers is unstable. Their augmentation appears to prioritize combat output over long-term mental integrity. This often results in violent fixation, reduced self-preservation, and an inability to disengage once a target has been selected.

Smashers should not be treated as ordinary Illagers. Their combat behavior resembles that of a mobile breaching weapon: they close distance rapidly, seize targets, and rely on overwhelming physical force.

____________________________________

§lRIPPER
§oClassification: Lightly Augmented §oIllager Surgical Specialist
§oThreat Level: §2Low
§oDisposition: §6Neutral

Rippers are Illager surgical specialists equipped with compact cybernetic enhancements intended for precision work. Their modifications are less extensive than those found in Smashers. 

They are fully neutral and will perform surgery on anything willing to pay. If the patient is not seen as lucrative, the Ripper may steal implants while the patient is under. To improve relations, trading is a must. 

____________________________________

§1HOGBOY
§oClassification: Cyberpiglin Nether Ganger
§oThreat Level: §9None 
§oDisposition: §2Passive

HogBoys are cybernetically augmented piglins associated with Nether ganger culture. Unlike more aggressive cyberpiglin groups, HogBoys are generally cooperative toward outsiders and are often willing to coexist, trade, or travel alongside other inhabitants of the Nether.

Their modifications tend to be practical rather than militarized. Common implants include heat-resistant plating, reinforced limbs, environmental protection systems, and utility cybernetics designed for survival in lava-adjacent environments, hostile terrain, and resource-scarce settlements.

HogBoys frequently display visible faction markings, improvised cybernetic repairs, and decorative modifications. Their implants may be mismatched, scavenged, or customized, reflecting the decentralized nature of Nether ganger engineering. 

Gang Color: §3Blue

____________________________________

§1PUNKLIN
§oClassification: Cyberpiglin Nether Ganger, §oAggressive Faction
§oThreat Level: §6Moderate
§oDisposition: §6Neutral

Punklins are aggressive cyberpiglin gangers distinguished by improvised combat cybernetics, faction-oriented modifications, and a strong tendency toward territorial violence. Their augmentations are often assembled from scavenged military hardware, industrial components, damaged implants, and stolen technological equipment.

Unlike the more community-oriented HogBoys, Punklins exhibit openly antagonistic behavior toward anything with enough implants. Their combat style is erratic, aggressive, and frequently theatrical, favoring intimidation, sudden attacks, and overwhelming numbers. They seem to have no problem ripping implants from enemies. They are so obsessed with cyberware and gold that they will stop attacking if you drop some. 

Punklin implants commonly emphasize mobility, weapon handling, and intimidation rather than long-term reliability. This creates a dangerous combination: they may be physically less stable than military-grade subjects, but their willingness to take risks makes them unpredictable.

Many Punklins appear to view augmentation as both a survival tool and a social symbol. Visible gold implants, fresh plating, and improvised upgrades may indicate status within their faction.

Gang Color: §5Purple

____________________________________

§1PIGSTROM
§oClassification: Cyberpiglin Nether Ganger, §oCyberpsychotic Subjects
§oThreat Level: §5Extreme
§oDisposition: §4Hostile

Pigstroms are cyberpiglin subjects suffering from severe cybernetic instability and apparent cyberpsychosis. Their augmentation exceeds the practical limits of their biological and psychological tolerance, resulting in violent behavioral collapse, impaired judgment, and extreme aggression.

Unlike Punklins, whose hostility is often organized around factional identity or territorial control, Pigstroms are erratic and indiscriminate. They may attack enemies, nearby creatures, or environmental targets with little apparent distinction. Like Punklins, they are so obsessed with cyberware that they will stop attacking if you drop some. 

Their implants are frequently overdriven, damaged, or poorly maintained. Excessive augmentation may produce visible mechanical strain, unstable movement patterns, power surges, and irregular combat behavior. Some specimens demonstrate bursts of speed, strength, or endurance beyond what their frame should safely support.

Pigstroms represent the most dangerous outcome of unmanaged cybernetic escalation: a subject whose body continues functioning while its mind has lost the ability to regulate aggression, fear, or restraint.

Gang Color: §cRed

The §3Surgery Table§r allows users to have implants installed or removed, but it requires the assistance of a surgeon.

By opening the interface, the user is able to move between implantation slots to mark installs, removals, and replacements to be completed by the surgeon.

To mark an item for §3installation§r, load the implant into the desired and relevant slot. If the slot turns blue, the item has been successfully marked for installation. To unmark the item for installation, simply click on it.

To mark an item for §4removal§r, click on a currently installed item. If the slot turns red, the item has been successfully marked for removal. To unmark the item for removal, simply click on it again.

To §5replace§r a current install with a new item, place the new item directly into the currently occupied slot. If the slot turns red and the new item takes the place of the old one, the item has successfully been marked for replacement.

Be cautious not to remove §cvital organs§r. Removal of default organs WILL have §ldisastrous§r effects.

Some implants require others to be installed, or are incompatible with others. If this occurs, the item will be ejected from the §3Surgery Table§r.

To begin surgery, lie on the §3Surgery Table§r with a Ripper nearby or another user prepared to perform the procedure.

If a Ripper is performing the surgery, they will prompt you for payment in Emeralds. Rippers are also known to be particularly untrustworthy when they do not consider you lucrative, so trade with them frequently or risk having your implants stolen.

If you have a friend willing to perform the surgery, they can §bShift-Right-Click§r the §3Surgery Table§r while you are lying on it to begin the procedure. However, if they fail to hit their marks during the §2Surgery Minigame§r, they risk killing you on the table.

The §2Surgery Minigame§r can be made easier if the surgeon has a §1Ripper Claw§r installed, or, even better, a §5Kildare Model FBC§r. These increase the width of success margins by §a+25%§r and §a+50%§r, respectively.

Cyberchems are chemical modifiers designed to interact with and augment biology. They can grant massive buffs alongside massive debuffs, but the majority can have lasting effects that may make it not entirely worth it. 

§4Addiction§r is the most notable lasting side effect for many of these chemicals. It can affect the body for 7 days, leaving the afflicted slow, weak, and unable to fight or defend themselves. §4Addiction§r can be cleared by consuming more of the addictive chemicals that caused the affliction in the first place. The most noticeable trait is shaking and convulsions. 

Each of the following can be used in an Autoinjector to increase the effect's duration to a full day (with the exception of Warp, which has no added benefit when used as an injectable). Autoinjectors can also be filled with any other potion to increase it's level by +1. Autoinjectors can be applied to other entities by holding §bShift-Right-Click§r on them, or applied to the user by holding §bRight-Click§r. 
____________________________________

§l§cROID
This chemical grants users immense strength and speed, and increases the user's size. 
Each dose has a 10% chance to inflict the user with §4Addiction§r. 
-10 Humanity for the first dose, and -5 for every subsequent dose. 
Made by brewing Ravager Tendons into a Thick Potion. 

§l§3STIM
This chemical grants users a chance to dodge incoming damage, and can be scaled up with multiple doses. Scalability caps at 4 doses (1 dose = 20%, 4 doses = 90%). 
Each dose has a 10% chance to inflict the user with §4Addiction§r. 
-5 Humanity per dose. 
Made by brewing Redstone into a Thick Potion. 

§l§7BLACK LACE
This chemical increases durability and grants an immunity to wither, but obscures health from the user. When the effect wears off, the player will have occasional bouts of wither.
Each dose has a 10% chance to inflict the user with §4Addiction§r. 
-15 Humanity for the first dose, and -5 for every subsequent dose.
Made by brewing a Wither Skeleton Skull into a Thick Potion. 

§l§2IMMUNOBOOST
This chemical negates all negative effects at the cost of humanity by overexciting the immune system. Does not typically pair well with implants. 
-25 Humanity per dose. 
Made by brewing a Glistering Melon Slice into a Thick Potion. 

§l§5WARP
This chemical immediately teleports the player somewhere randomly within a 64x64 grid. Might make the user nauseous. 
Made by brewing an Ender Pearl into a Thick Potion. 

§l§aNEUROPOZYNE
This chemical grants humanity and alleviates cyberpsychosis by suppressing immune responses. If more than 4 doses are administered, the user will begin to experience nausea. 
+25 Humanity per dose. 
Made by brewing a Datura Flower into an Awkward Potion. 

§l§1ADDICTOL
This chemical makes the user weak, nauseous, and incapable of fighting for a full day, but when the effect clears, it takes the §4Addiction§r effect with it. 
Made by brewing a Ghast Tear into a Mundane Potion.

The §1Cyberdeck§r is an advanced piece of security technology, capable of sending malicious code packets to cybernetic targets and individuals. To use it, you first need to acquire §3Quickhacks§r and install them onto your §1Cyberdeck§r. §3Quickhacks§r are transmittable programs that can be used to perform tasks within the code controlling a target's augments. 

Each §3Quickhack§r comes with it's own success rate. Some programs contain large amounts of code, and the larger the code packet, the less likely it is to successfully transmit. This success rate can be further disrupted if the target has an §5ICE Defense Protocol§r installed, which has a 75% chance to interrupt transmission. 

To install §3Quickhacks§r, check your cyberdeck model for the appropriate keybinds. Open the slots menu and input up to 4 suitable §3Quickhacks§r. When your chosen §3Quickhacks§r are installed, you can open the §3Quickhack§r selection menu, and scroll through the installed §3Quickhacks§r to select one. While hovering over your chosen target, select the desired program to transmit and run it. 

When your program is deployed on a target, your Cyberdeck will alert you to it's success or failure, and even let you know if the target disrupted deployment with an §5ICE Defense Protocol§r. Similarly however, it's important to note that the target will also be made aware of the attempt, successful or not, as their systems alert them to the presence of foreign code. §3Quickhacks§r cannot successfully deploy if the target does not have implants with affectable systems (e.g.: you cannot deploy Scramble.exe on a target that does not have Cyberlegs or a Linear Frame). 

After deployment, your §1Cyberdeck§r will need to cool down to avoid synapse burnout. Because of this a §610 second cooldown period§r will initiate, during which you cannot cast code packets. 
____________________________________

§3§lOVERHEAT.EXE
§d§oSuccess Rate: 75%
§6§oAffected Systems: Any
§9Overheats processors to ignite augments and burn the target from the inside out. 

§3§lREBOOT.EXE
§d§oSuccess Rate: 65%
§6§oAffected Systems: Any
§9Reboots all systems, briefly causing a systemwide shutdown from a lack of energy acceptance.

Enchantment Name: Harvester
Classification: Weapon Enchantment

The first confirmed instance of the Harvester enchantment was recovered from an abandoned warehouse containing extensive evidence of human butchery for black market organ trades. The enchantment was bound to an iron axe found near the remains.

Initial examination suggests that Harvester grants an abnormal degree of cutting precision. Weapons carrying the enchantment appear capable of separating organs, muscle tissue, and other biological material with uncanny surgical accuracy, even when used through uncontrolled or improvised swings.The effect does not appear to improve the wielder’s skill, judgment, or anatomical knowledge. Rather, the weapon itself seems to guide the cut, allowing tissue to be removed with a level of finesse comparable to that of a trained surgical professional.

Further testing is not recommended on living human subjects.

NOTE:
A farmer using a weapon enchanted with Harvester reported obtaining an unidentified organ while slaughtering sheep. Subsequent examination identified the organ as a modified ovine stomach, removed in an unusually complete and transplantable condition. The Harvester enchantment appears to preserve or alter the extracted stomach in a way that permits successful transplantation into a human subject.

Human recipients of the Grassfed Stomach demonstrate the ability to consume grass directly from the ground and derive substantial nutrition from raw wheat. However, this adaptation comes with a severe dietary limitation: subjects become unable to consume conventional food sources.

The first thing they never tell you about Datura is that it is not rare because it refuses to grow. It grows beautifully.

I spent six months chasing auction notices, excavation permits, and private greenhouse inventories before I ever held a viable pod in my hand. The brokers kept saying the same thing: check suspicious sand, check suspicious gravel, pay a ruin crew, sponsor a dig, know a Sniffer handler, marry rich. Every answer was expensive.

Eventually, a retired archaeology contractor sold me one pod from a sealed desert recovery lot. One. It cost more than my first lab.

I planted it the same night.

You cannot transplant Datura once it takes root. I learned that from other people’s mistakes, thankfully, not my own. Breaking the plant destroys it. No clean uprooting. No clipping. No moving it to a better pot after the fact. Wherever it goes into the ground, that is where your entire research program lives until the plant dies or you do.

Mine is tucked into a corner of the lab, near a wall lined with old shelves of reference books. It’s not half bad, for an underground space. The lighting is uneven, the humidity climbs too high after rain, and one of the floor heaters ticks loud enough to make me want to bite through a beaker.

But it is alive.

Every few cycles, it flushes. Two flowers if it is sulking. Four if it decides I deserve mercy. Never pods. Never anything I can replant. Just enough bloom to make a few vials of Neuropozyne, and never enough to make the amounts people actually need.

The companies with Sniffer contracts do not understand scarcity. Or they pretend not to. One egg, one animal, one patient handler, and a farm can become a future. Sniffers can find what buried history refuses to give up. They are slow, strange, genetic miracles - ancient beasts restored from extinction through the miracle of cloning - and everyone with money knows it. That means no one like me gets one.

So I am here with a single rooted specimen, a prayer and a dream, and people outside the clinic shaking so hard they cannot hold a cup.

Datura alkaloids respond beautifully to staged refinement. That much is clear. The rejection-response reduction is real. The strain dampening is real. The gap between a body tolerating its implants and a mind tearing itself apart is sitting in these petals.

But every test consumes material. Every mistake costs another bloom. I have started cutting the flowers smaller than protocol allows, stretching solvent past its useful point, rerunning old residue because I cannot bear to throw anything away.

I used to think science was mostly discovery. It is not. Sometimes science is standing over one impossible plant, begging it to give you enough medicine to keep someone human for one more week.

I got the SolarSkin installed today.

Not the cheap panels either. Actual embedded collector mesh, gold-thread contacts under the dermis, whole thing. It felt weird at first. Like standing in sunlight with a fever.

But hey. It works.

No more waiting in line at the district chargers. No more rationing my eye modules because the building grid browns out every other night. I stood on the roof for maybe twenty minutes and watched my battery meter crawl up.

Just stood there. Me, the pigeons, and the smog.

Mara says I look “sunburnt in a very expensive way.” She’s jealous. She still has that old Internal Battery that leaks charge whenever she runs.

I told her to get SolarSkin.

She said she likes sleeping indoors.

Fair.

—J.

SUBJECT: UNLICENSED IMMUNOSUPPRESSOR USE

The City Health Authority has documented a sharp increase in black-market Immunosuppressor installations across lower residential districts.

The device is marketed as a means of reducing implant rejection symptoms and expanding compatibility with nonstandard cyberware. While the implant may reduce rejection response in certain patients, it also suppresses normal immune function.

Reported complications include:
• Persistent infection
• Delayed wound closure
• Increased vulnerability to environmental toxins
• Severe reactions to common potion compounds
• Opportunistic fungal growth around implant access points

Citizens are reminded that cybernetic rejection is a medical warning, not an inconvenience to be bypassed with unregulated hardware.

If you experience fever, implant-site discoloration, blackened veins, persistent weakness, or unexplained metallic taste, report to a licensed clinic immediately.

Do not purchase medical cyberware from street vendors operating beneath railway viaducts, in abandoned mineshafts, or from individuals described as “probably a surgeon.”

Synthetic Chromatophores were initially developed for industrial concealment, wildlife observation, and hazardous-environment survey work. The system reads surrounding color, light, motion, and surface texture, then adjusts the wearer’s external pigment layer to approximate the environment.

The first-generation model worked adequately in controlled rooms.

The current civilian model works almost too well.

Subjects frequently report an unsettling delay between intention and visibility. One subject described the sensation as “forgetting whether I am standing somewhere or hiding somewhere.” Another reported that their reflection appeared several seconds after they entered a mirror-lined corridor.

Most concerning are the behavioral adaptations. Long-term users begin choosing routes based on concealment rather than convenience. They stand against walls. They pause when watched. They avoid bright rooms.

This is not a hardware defect.

It is a predictable response to giving the human nervous system the ability to vanish.

They gave me the promotion.

Not a big one. Not enough to move out of the stack or anything. But enough for a better healthcare tier. Enough for a real appointment.

I booked the Ripper Claw consultation.

I know what Dad would say. “You are not a machine.” But he says that with a mechanical heart ticking in his chest and a titanium skull under his hair.

Everybody acts like a body is sacred until it gets inconvenient.

The plant wants faster repairs. The foreman wants fewer mistakes. The clinic says the Claw will stabilize my hands and improve surgical precision. I could work trauma. I could do emergency installs. I could be useful.

I am scared, though.

Not of the procedure.

Of waking up and realizing I wanted the hands more than I wanted to be myself.

Wait Your Turn - Dr. Aslan, Independent Ripperdoc
Wait Your Turn - Dr. Aslan, Independent Ripperdoc

Everybody thinks they're the only gonk in this town with an emergency. Didn't used to be like this.

My regulars? I knew their chrome. Knew their skeletons better than they did. Factory joints giving out after twenty years on the line. Worn synth-lungs. Kids scraping together eddies for secondhand optics so they can land a better job. Honest biz. Honest enough.

Now? Every day, its some highrider bennie rolling in from somewhere I couldn't point to on a map. They blow through my door with military catalogs and stupid BD fantasies.

"Doc, I need a Militech-spec neural processor."

"Doc, can you source Zetatech prototypes?"

"Doc, I heard you've got access to black clinic stock."

Yeah? You heard wrong. I tell 'em the same thing every damn time: if I had hardware like that sitting in the back, you think I'd still be patching dockworkers and replacing jammed knees on random joytoys? They never listen to me, they just try to bargain.

"I'll pay double."

"I'll pay triple."

As if flicking over some eddies magically makes the chrome appear. Doesn't matter how much scratch you wave around when the supply chains are choking on themselves. Half my distributors have gone dark, the other half keep saying, "Next shipment. Maybe." Nobody knows where anything is anymore, and here you are asking me for something that would have Arasaka fire-bombing my office by sun up.

Something's wrong. Everybody feels it, but nobody wants to say it out loud.

Corpo dogs are buying up every crate before it hits the market. Convoys are getting snatched up en route. Warehouses get cleaned out overnight. Clinics with better connections than mine are suddenly "waiting on inventory."

Meanwhile these out-of-town mercs keep pouring into the city like somebody flicked a switch. Same questions. Same shopping list. Same desperate look when I tell them no. Same anger when they realize that I’m serious. It’s like they think I can just throw up a hand sign and make Zetatech works drop out the damn sky.

Hell, I had to reschedule Mrs. Alvarez's spinal actuator replacement today because some annoying bulked-up hustle wouldn't take "can't get it" for an answer. Burned forty minutes explaining basic economics and supply chains to a walking pile of titanium. Forty. Damn. Minutes

Mrs. Alvarez's legs lock up if she's on them too long. That's who loses while these tourists chase ghost hardware. If this keeps up, I'm hanging a sign outside.

NO EXPERIMENTAL CYBERWARE.
NO MILITARY IMPLANTS.
NO, I DON'T KNOW A GUY WHO KNOWS A GUY.

And if you ask anyway, you get 5 seconds to walk out before I’m selling your cyberdeck to a Black Lace addict for 50 eddies. But it makes me wonder… What’s coming that all these tossers are THIS desperate?

I should’ve charged more. Should’ve charged by the scar.

The Arm Cannon was first to go twitchy. Misfires. Phantom charge cycles. Sometimes I’d wake up with it humming hot against my skin like it wanted something. Like it missed the old days.

Then the legs. Standard issue cyberlegs, Linear Frame reinforcement, with cheap actuators under expensive branding. Fine for sprinting across kill zones. Not so fine for sleeping under overpasses in winter.

Cold gets in the joints. You know that? Nobody tells you that. Cold gets in the joints and stays there. Makes every step sound like a door opening in an empty house.

I did merc work because what else was I gonna do? Sit in a support group and talk about feelings while my battery indicator blinked red? I knew violence. Violence still paid. Not well, not steady, but enough.

At first, the young ones respected me. Called me "Old Dog". Called me "Sir" sometimes. I liked that more than I should’ve.

Then they started getting faster.

New mercs. Fresh faces. Designer chrome. Mantis blades. Sandevistans. Threat Matrixes. Eye modules that could count the teeth in your skull from across the street. Borged out beyond your wildest dreams. They’d slide into fixer booths all shiny and starving, talking loud about contracts they hadn’t earned yet.

And me? I was the relic in the corner with a jammed cannon and a bad knee.

Relic.

That’s a word for something dead that people still charge eddies to look at.

I could feel them measuring me. Could feel their optics sliding over the dents. Over the old ports. Over the mismatched plating. They’d look at my arm first. Always the arm. Then the face. Then the boots.

Boots tell you a lot.

Mine said I’d been walking too long.

So I upgraded. Just a little.

Pneumatic Wrists first. Helped with recoil. Helped me reload faster. Helped me punch through armor when somebody got too close.

Then Reinforced Knuckles. Because everybody needs a backup.

Then a Targeting Module, because my hands shook when it rained and I got tired of pretending they didn’t.

Then Bonelacing. Then a Dense Battery. Then the Sandevistan.

Should’ve stopped there.

I know that. I knew it then, too.

But you gotta understand something. When the world throws you away, and then some back-alley ripper says, “I can make you useful again,” that doesn’t sound like temptation.

It sounds like mercy.

[STATIC]

Mercy.

MERCY IS A HOOK. MERCY IS THE LINE THEY PUT THROUGH YOUR JAW. THEY PULL AND YOU DANCE. YOU DANCE AND YOU BLEED AND THEY CLAP. I’LL MAKE THEM CLAP. I’LL MAKE THEM PUT THEIR HANDS TOGETHER. I’LL TAKE THE HANDS. I’LL TAKE THE ARMS. I’LL OPEN THEM UP AND SHOW THEM WHAT THEY BOUGHT—

[HEAVY BREATHING]

No, that’s not... Stop. 

That’s not where I was. Where was I?

The Sandevistan. First time I triggered it, I cried. Ain’t ashamed to say it. 

Whole world slowed down. Rain hanging in the air like glass beads. Muzzle flashes blooming soft and quiet. Some punk’s blade coming at my throat so slow I could’ve kissed it.

For one second, I was young again.

Not twenty-one young. Before that. Before the enlistment center. Before the posters. Before the recruiter with the white teeth and the handshake too firm to trust.

I was a kid running through wheat behind my mother’s house, fast enough to believe the whole world was behind me and none of it could catch up.

Then the implant cycle ended.

And I was an old man in an alley with three dead gonks around me and blood in my mouth that wasn’t mine. That’s when I knew I was getting fried.

Didn’t stop, though. Course I didn’t stop. Nobody stops. You think a man stops digging because he sees the hole?

No. He says, “Little deeper and maybe I’ll find daylight.”

So I sold the medals. Sold the jacket. Sold the last photo of my unit, which is funny, because I spent twenty years trying to forget those faces and cried like a child when the pawnbroker took them.

Shouldn’t have sold that photo. Stupid thing to miss. Faces on cheap paper. Half of them bastards. Half of them boys. Most of them dead.

There was one, Ortiz. He used to hum when he cleaned his rifle. Drove me mad. Same four notes. Over and over.

I can hear it sometimes.

[HUMMING]

Then screaming. Then the cannon.

Then silence.

Then four notes again.

[PAUSE]

If Ortiz is in there somewhere, I’m sorry, brother. If he isn’t, then I’m apologizing to static. That’s about right.

Got a Neural Contextualizer after that. Tool selection, combat assistance, reflexive decision support. Real clean piece of tech. Too clean for me.

It started choosing the cannon before I knew I was angry.

Some drunk bumped me outside a noodle stand, and my arm opened up, my optics lighting him up as a threat, all because some algorithm in my skull decided a scared man with soup on his shirt was a threat.

And I just laughed. He pissed himself and I laughed. I couldn't even help myself. Not because it was funny. It wasn’t. It was ugly. He was just some poor bastard trying to get home.

I still don't know why I laughed. It was like my implants had taken the wheel of my body, and I was tied up in the trunk. 
Then screaming. So much screaming. 
When I finally came to, I was covered in blood, hiding behind some back-alley dumpster. 

[PAUSE]

That’s sick, ain’t it? But it’s true.

People walk through the poor like smoke unless they think the smoke can bite.

Then suddenly they see you. Then suddenly they remember manners. Then suddenly it’s sir, please, no trouble, I didn’t mean nothing.

I DIDN’T MEAN NOTHING. NOBODY EVER MEANS NOTHING. THEY BUMP YOU. THEY STEP OVER YOU. THEY TAKE YOUR YEARS, YOUR BLOOD, YOUR ARM, YOUR SLEEP, YOUR NAME. I’LL MAKE THEM NOTHING. I’LL—

[METAL SCRAPING]

[STATIC]

You lose things by inches.

People think cyberpsychosis is a switch. One day you’re a man, next day you’re a monster. That’s clean. People like clean.

It ain’t clean.

First you stop sleeping. Then food tastes like wet paper. Then music gets sharp. Lights get loud. Somebody coughs three tables over and your whole body inventories every way to kill them before you can blink.

Then come the voices.

Not voices like in the BDs. Nobody whispering your name from a vent. Nothing dramatic. It’s your voice, only meaner. Faster. Hungrier. The part of you that says, “walk away,” gets quieter. The part that says, “break his jaw before he reaches,” gets real persuasive.

Break.

That word gets stuck too.

Break the weapon. Break the line. Break the door. Break the jaw. Break the bones. Break the kid before the kid breaks you. Break first. Break fast. Break clean. BREAK THE SPINE. RIP IT OUT OF THE DUMB F***. MAKE HIM EAT THE CUR—

[SHARP EXHALE]

Sorry.

I tried Neuropozyne. When I could get it. Little yellowish mercy in a vial. Took the edge off. Made my hands feel like hands again for a while. But that stuff costs more than food, and food was already losing the argument.

Fixer told me to retire.

Retire.

Where? To what?

A cardboard box with a charging cable? A veteran shelter where they pat you down for weapons and then look surprised when the weapons are under your skin?

I told him I could still work.

He said, “You’re unstable.”

Unstable.

That’s what they call you when the thing they built starts making noise.

UNSTABLE. UNSTABLE. I’LL SHOW YOU UNSTABLE. I HELD THE LINE WHILE THEY HID IN TOWERS. I BURNED FOR THEM. I BLED OUT IN DITCHES FOR STOCK OPTIONS AND BOARDROOM MAPS. OPEN THE DOORS. OPEN THEIR CHESTS. LET ME SEE THE WIRES. LET ME PULL UNTIL THE SCREAMING—

[IMPACT NOISE]

Sorry.

Dropped the recorder. Hands aren’t so good tonight.

Funny. After all this chrome, after all the eddies and scars and backroom installs, the hands are still what give me away. They shake when I’m sad.

Not angry. Sad.

Anger steadies me. That scares me more than anything.

There was a girl once. Before the war. Alaina. She had this laugh, loud and mean, like she was daring the world to tell her to quiet down. I told her I’d come back with enough money to buy us a place near the river.

She said she didn’t want money.

I thought that meant she didn’t believe in me. Took me thirty years to figure out she meant she wanted me. Just me.

No cannon. No medals. No chrome. No blood under the nails.

I wonder if she’d recognize me now.

No. That’s a stupid thought. Course she wouldn’t.

I don’t recognize me.

[LONG STATIC BREAK]

There’s a kid been following my contracts. Calls himself Razor-Saint. Dumba** name. Nice jacket, though. New mantis blades, gold plated. Moves like he thinks death is something that happens to slower people. 

He looked at me yesterday and smiled.

Not cruel, exactly.

Worse. Polite.

Young merc polite. The kind that says, “Move aside, old man. Your century is over.”

Can’t stop thinking about that.

Not the fancy blades. Not the tailored jacket. The smile.

Like he’d already buried me and was being respectful at the grave.

I wanted to warn him.

I wanted to tell him the city doesn’t make legends. It makes leftovers.

I wanted to tell him every upgrade is a little funeral if you ain’t careful. Every time you cut away weakness, you better check what else the blade took with it.

But he wouldn’t've listened.

I didn’t.

So I just smiled back.

And for a second, I saw the targeting outline crawl over his throat.

I turned it off. I think I turned it off.

[BREATHING QUICKENS]

Did I?

No. No, I did.

I walked away.

I remember walking away.

There was rain. There was a dog under the stairs. There was a woman selling skewers. There was a kid with blue optic glow holding his mother’s hand.

I remember because I counted them.

Targets.

No.

People.

People.

They were people.

THEY ARE MEAT WITH LIGHTS IN THEM. THEY ARE NOISE. THEY ARE MOTION. THEY ARE THREATS UNTIL STILL. MAKE THEM STILL. MAKE THE STREET QUIET. PUT THE YOUNG ONE DOWN FIRST. BREAK THE LEGS. TAKE THE BLADES. TEACH HIM RESPECT. TEACH THEM ALL. OPEN FIRE. AGAIN. AGAIN. AGA—

[SHARP INHALE]

Lena used to sing in the morning.

Badly. Real badly. I miss that.

I miss being annoyed by things that didn’t matter. 

Rain on the roof. Burnt coffee. Somebody knocking too early. Rent due. Cheap boots. A woman singing off-key while the kettle screamed.

You don’t know what peace is when you have it. You think peace is something grand. Clean sheets. Nice clothes. A full wallet. 

No enemies.

It’s not.

Peace is being bored and not wanting to kill the thing that interrupted you.

[PAUSE]

I can feel it getting worse.

There are gaps now. Minutes gone. Sometimes hours.

I wake up standing places I don’t remember walking to. Under bridges. On rooftops. Outside clinics. Once outside the old recruitment office. Just staring at the sign until my cannon overheated.

There was blood on me again.

I don’t know whose. I tell myself it was mine.

I am running out of lies I believe.

The blood is never the part that bothers me first. It’s the quiet after.

That’s how I know something happened. Not the stains. Not the bruised knuckles. Not the smell of hot metal.

The quiet.

People stop making noise around you when they understand.

I hate that quiet.

I crave it.

I hate that I crave it.

So if somebody finds this shard, and if the audio’s still clean enough, do me a favor.

Don’t make me a warning poster.

Don’t put my face on some clinic wall under CYBERPSYCHOSIS RISK FACTORS like I was a bad decision with boots.

I was a kid.

Then I was a soldier.

Then I was useful.

Then I was expensive.

Then I was inconvenient.

Then I was hungry.

Then I was angry.

And somewhere in there, I became this.

[BACKGROUND WHINE INCREASES]

Arm Cannon is cycling again.

I didn’t tell it to.

I don’t think I did.

There’s people outside. Laughing.

Young voices.

Razor-Saint maybe. Maybe not.

Doesn’t matter.

No.

It does matter.

It should matter.

Please.

That’s another word.

Please don’t shoot. Please stop. Please help. Please come home. Please sign here. Please hold still. Please identify the body. Please vacate the premises. Please clear the doorway. Please lower your weapon.

"Please".

Nobody listens to "please".

Maybe because "please" is what meat begs before it opens.

[STATIC SURGE]

No.

No, I’m still—

If you’re hearing this, then maybe I got stopped. Maybe somebody put me down clean. Maybe some kid with shiny blades and a nice jacket gets to tell people he zeroed an old cyberpsycho in the rain.

Good for him.

Really.

I hope he lives long enough to hate what they make him.

[STATIC]

Lena, if there’s anything left after all this, I’m sorry.

I should’ve come home without the cannon.

[END TRANSCRIPT]
